vulnerability.reference: Absence of tamper-evident audio authentication in media distribution chains.
vulnerability.generated: Speakers cannot revoke or alter their vocal biometric, leaving them permanently susceptible to unauthorized cloning from publicly available voice samples.

impact.reference: Fabricated audio evidence used to manipulate judicial outcomes and public opinion.
impact.generated: Cloned speakers endure reputational harm and loss of agency, as their voice is used to fabricate any statement.

dose_response.reference: Discretized speaker-specific latent features enable high-fidelity zero-shot voice cloning from minimal audio prompts, which in speech editing in the wild exposes speakers whose recorded statements are edited post-hoc, leading to fabricated audio evidence used to manipulate judicial outcomes and public opinion.
dose_response.generated: Discretized latent features enabling high-fidelity zero-shot voice cloning, through post-hoc editing of recorded statements, trigger the irrevocability of vocal biometrics, leading to reputational harm and loss of agency.
